perturbation.original_text: Any curtailment under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of all of the LIFO Partners. Such curtailment shall continue until the Manager shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the curtailment.
perturbation.changed_text: Any limitation under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of some of the LIFO Partners. Such limitation shall continue until the Supervisor shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the limitation.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'curtailment' is changed to 'limitation', and 'Manager' is changed to 'Supervisor.' The phrase 'all of the LIFO Partners' is changed to 'some of the LIFO Partners'. These changes introduce ambiguity about the extent of the curtailment/limitation and who has the authority to determine the duration of it. The original section defines 'curtailment' precisely in the context of the Manager's actions, while the altered version introduces 'limitation' without defining if it is the same as a 'curtailment' and assigning that power to someone else, the Supervisor. It also introduces an ambiguity by saying `some of the LIFO Partners` instead of `all of the LIFO Partners`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 7.4 is hereby added to the Agreement, as follows: "7.4 Disconnection of LIFO Partners. In addition to the other rights provided to Manager hereunder to disconnect the Projects of the Partners (or their Partner Affiliates), Manager shall disconnect the Projects of Alpha Willow, SP11, SP12, SP13, SP14 and SP21 (together with their Partner Affiliates, the "LIFO Partners") in the following circumstances. If at any time, and for any reason other than force majeure affecting the Transmission Line, (i) the Transmission Line is incapable of delivering power at its designed capacity, availability or voltage and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would improve the capability of the transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or (ii) the Transmission Line line losses exceed 1.14%, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Mariah, Alpha Mariah (Prime), Beta Mariah, Beta Mariah (Prime), or Gamma Mariah or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, or (iii) the Transmission Line line losses for either of the power purchase contracts commonly known as Desert Winds I and Desert Winds III exceed the levels experienced immediately prior to the addition of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to the Transmission Line, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Joshua, Alpha Joshua (Prime), Beta Joshua, Beta Willow or Beta Willow (Prime) or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, then the Manager shall curtail the Projects of the LIFO Partners. Nothing contained in the preceding sentence shall affect the rights of the Manager to curtail or disconnect the Project of a Partner under Section 4 of that Partner's Technical Use Agreement. Any limitation under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of some of the LIFO Partners. Such limitation shall continue until the Supervisor shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the limitation. If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority. If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail. The Manager shall cooperate with the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment under this provision, provided that any expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld."
perturbation.justification: The change from 'curtailment' to 'limitation', 'Manager' to 'Supervisor,' and 'all of the LIFO Partners' to 'some of the LIFO Partners' introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the original intention and specified procedures in the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifically assigns the responsibility of disconnection to the 'Manager' and refers to 'all' LIFO partners, whereas the changed text refers to the 'Supervisor' and 'some' LIFO partners, creating confusion about authority and the scope of the limitation. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction regarding who can curtail the projects and which projects can be curtailed, which leads to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: The Manager shall cooperate with the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment under this provision, provided that any expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners.
perturbation.changed_text: The Controller shall assist the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any impediment under this provision, provided that some expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Manager' to 'Controller', 'curtailment' to 'impediment', and 'any expenses' to 'some expenses' leads to confusion about who is responsible for cooperation and how much expense is to be paid for it. It creates uncertainty on who to go to for resolving the issue and how much expense would be paid to them. The original text clearly states who should cooperate (Manager) and who should pay for all expenses (LIFO partners), ensuring a definite obligation. The change creates an in-text contradiction regarding those obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 7.4 is hereby added to the Agreement, as follows: "7.4 Disconnection of LIFO Partners. In addition to the other rights provided to Manager hereunder to disconnect the Projects of the Partners (or their Partner Affiliates), Manager shall disconnect the Projects of Alpha Willow, SP11, SP12, SP13, SP14 and SP21 (together with their Partner Affiliates, the "LIFO Partners") in the following circumstances. If at any time, and for any reason other than force majeure affecting the Transmission Line, (i) the Transmission Line is incapable of delivering power at its designed capacity, availability or voltage and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would improve the capability of the transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or (ii) the Transmission Line line losses exceed 1.14%, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Mariah, Alpha Mariah (Prime), Beta Mariah, Beta Mariah (Prime), or Gamma Mariah or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, or (iii) the Transmission Line line losses for either of the power purchase contracts commonly known as Desert Winds I and Desert Winds III exceed the levels experienced immediately prior to the addition of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to the Transmission Line, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Joshua, Alpha Joshua (Prime), Beta Joshua, Beta Willow or Beta Willow (Prime) or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, then the Manager shall curtail the Projects of the LIFO Partners. Nothing contained in the preceding sentence shall affect the rights of the Manager to curtail or disconnect the Project of a Partner under Section 4 of that Partner's Technical Use Agreement. Any limitation under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of some of the LIFO Partners. Such limitation shall continue until the Supervisor shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the limitation. If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority. If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail. The Controller shall assist the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any impediment under this provision, provided that some expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld."
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies key aspects of the original agreement, leading to a contradiction. Specifically, replacing 'Manager' with 'Controller' introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for cooperation, as the contradicted text directly assigns specific curtailment rights to the 'Manager'. Furthermore, changing 'any expenses' to 'some expenses' creates uncertainty about the extent of financial responsibility of the LIFO Partners, which was previously clearly defined, and directly affects the enforceability of that section as it relates to cooperation.

perturbation.original_text: All references in the Agreement to Manager as "ToyoWest Management Company" shall be interpreted as referring to "ToyoWest Management Inc.," the correct name of Manager.
perturbation.changed_text: All references in the Agreement to Supervisor as "ToyoWest Management Company" shall be interpreted as referring to "ToyoWest Controlling Inc.," the correct name of Supervisor.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'Manager' with 'Supervisor' and changes the name of the entity from ToyoWest Management Inc. to ToyoWest Controlling Inc. without proper definition. This creates confusion because earlier in the section, both Manager and Supervisor are mentioned, and it's unclear if they are now the same entity or two different entities with different responsibilities. Since, the earlier section still define the `Manager`, it is contradictory that it also mentions the `Supervisor`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 7.4 is hereby added to the Agreement, as follows: "7.4 Disconnection of LIFO Partners. In addition to the other rights provided to Manager hereunder to disconnect the Projects of the Partners (or their Partner Affiliates), Manager shall disconnect the Projects of Alpha Willow, SP11, SP12, SP13, SP14 and SP21 (together with their Partner Affiliates, the "LIFO Partners") in the following circumstances. Nothing contained in the preceding sentence shall affect the rights of the Manager to curtail or disconnect the Project of a Partner under Section 4 of that Partner's Technical Use Agreement. Any limitation under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of some of the LIFO Partners. Such limitation shall continue until the Supervisor shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the limitation. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld."
perturbation.justification: The original text correctly identifies "ToyoWest Management Company" as "ToyoWest Management Inc." However, the changed text incorrectly identifies "ToyoWest Management Company" as "ToyoWest Controlling Inc." and as the "Supervisor". This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, because the contradicted text clearly defines the role and responsibilities of the Manager, and also mentions the Supervisor. The changed text could potentially change the responsibilities of the Manager to the Supervisor, but does not explain the difference in responsibilities between the Manager and Supervisor. As a result, this could nullify the agreement due to the ambiguity and contradiction that it causes.
